objective: Close F-0056 by preserving the current question or material status during ordinary editor saves and allowing a disabled question to become available only through the existing explicit publishDraft entry. Do not add lifecycle commands, change authorization, or alter repository, schema, migration, Provider or mechanism behavior.
blockedFiles: new enable, publish, role, entitlement, authorization, quota or default capability
Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push

authoritativeChainEvidence.uiApiCallers: The two dedicated editors construct PATCH payloads. Both currently hard-code status=available; question has an existing explicit publishDraft flag, while material has no publish intent.
authoritativeChainEvidence.routeService: Existing question and material PATCH routes validate and persist the caller-supplied status. This task changes only the trusted editor payload and does not alter route, service or repository contracts.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: Current repositories already enforce expectedUpdatedAt and is_locked=false atomically, covering F-0055. No repository change is needed for F-0056.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schema: Existing available/disabled status values are sufficient. No schema or migration source is needed.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: Direct editor tests cover ordinary disabled question/material saves preserving disabled and the explicit AI-adopted question publish path still sending available.
authoritativeChainEvidence.requirements: Question and material disablement are explicit lifecycle states; ordinary editing must not imply re-enable. Existing publishDraft is the narrow explicit question publishing intent.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: No new enable command, material publish flow, F-0053 binding eligibility, F-0077 recommendation command, schema, database, permission, Provider, dependency or mechanism change.
